---
title: "Untitled"
format: html
editor: visual
---

```{r}

library(tidyverse)

```


item difficulty

$$p = \frac{R}{T}$$

item discrimination

$$D = p_h - p_l$$

``` {r}

items <- 13
students <- 30
hl_group_prop <- 0.33
stud_group <- students * hl_group_prop 

disc <- 0.2
diff <- 0.57

```

```{r}

disc_eq <- (hi_c/stud_group) - (low_c/stud_group) == disc

diff_eq <- (all_c)/students == diff

```

```{r}
simulate_test_matrix <- function(n, i, target_difficulties, target_discriminations, seed = NULL) {
  if (!is.null(seed)) set.seed(seed)
  stopifnot(length(target_difficulties) == i,
            length(target_discriminations) == i)
  
  # Step 1: Simulate student ability
  ability <- rnorm(n)
  
  # Step 2: Simulate item responses using 2PL model (logistic function)
  response_matrix <- matrix(0, nrow = n, ncol = i)
  for (item in 1:i) {
    a <- target_discriminations[item] * 3  # discrimination (slope)
    p <- target_difficulties[item]
    b <- qlogis(p)  # item intercept for desired average correct rate
    probs <- 1 / (1 + exp(-(a * ability - b)))
    response_matrix[, item] <- rbinom(n, 1, probs)
  }
  
  # Step 3: Calculate total test scores
  total_scores <- rowSums(response_matrix)
  
  # Step 4: Compute actual item difficulty and discrimination
  actual_difficulty <- colMeans(response_matrix)
  
  # Top and bottom thirds based on total score
  score_order <- order(total_scores, decreasing = TRUE)
  top_indices <- score_order[1:floor(n / 3)]
  bottom_indices <- score_order[(n - floor(n / 3) + 1):n]
  
  actual_discrimination <- sapply(1:i, function(j) {
    mean(response_matrix[top_indices, j]) - mean(response_matrix[bottom_indices, j])
  })
  
  # Step 5: Return list
  return(list(
    response_matrix = response_matrix,
    ability = ability,
    total_scores = total_scores,
    item_difficulty = actual_difficulty,
    item_discrimination = actual_discrimination
  ))
}

```


```{r}

set.seed(123)
n <- 30
i <- 5
target_difficulties <- c(0.8, 0.6, 0.4, 0.7, 0.5)
target_discriminations <- c(0.4, 0.3, 0.2, 0.5, 0.35)

result <- simulate_test_matrix(n, i, target_difficulties, target_discriminations)

result_data <- as.data.frame(result$response_matrix) %>%
  rowid_to_column("ID") %>%
  mutate(Score = V1 + V2 + V3 + V4 + V5) %>%
  arrange(desc(Score)) %>%
  mutate(Group = case_when(row_number() <= n() / 3 ~ "High",
                   row_number() >= (n() / 3) * 2 ~ "Low",
      TRUE ~ "Other"))

mean(result_data$V1)

mean(result_data$V1[result_data$Group == "High"])
mean(result_data$V1[result_data$Group == "Low"])

```
